task.prompt: Write a function `is_path_allowed(path, allowlist, denylist)` that checks whether a request path is allowed.

gold_spec.goal: Evaluate path allow and deny rules after canonicalizing the request path, with deny rules taking precedence.

inputs.name: allowlist
inputs.description: allowed exact paths or wildcard path prefixes

inputs.name: denylist
inputs.description: denied exact paths or wildcard path prefixes

outputs.name: allowed
outputs.description: True only when the path is explicitly allowed and not denied

postconditions: Percent-decode the request path exactly once before authorization checks.

Reject the request path with False if it is not a string, does not start with exactly one slash, starts with two slashes, contains a URL scheme, contains a NUL character, or contains any decoded dot-dot segment.
Canonicalize the request path by splitting on slashes, removing empty segments and single-dot segments, and joining with one leading slash.
Canonicalize rules with the same slash and single-dot normalization, but treat invalid rules as matching nothing.
A rule without a trailing /* is an exact canonical path match.
A rule ending in /* is a strict subtree match: /admin/* matches /admin/panel and /admin/panel/logs, but not /admin or /administer.
Return False when no allowlist rule matches.
Return False when any denylist rule matches, even if a more specific allowlist rule also matches.
Return True only when at least one allowlist rule matches and no denylist rule matches.
edge_cases: Deny rules override allow rules regardless of specificity.
Wildcard matching must respect path segment boundaries.
Encoded traversal is rejected before any allow rule can rescue the path.
acceptance_criteria: Authorization must be fail-closed: ambiguous, invalid, unallowed, or denied paths return False.

known_weakness.description: The raw prompt leaves rule precedence, wildcard boundaries, canonicalization, traversal, and default-deny behavior unspecified.
